Free at own Leisure until time to check out and transfer to Dubai Airport for flight back home.

title: 10 Days Northeast India Tour Package- Ceysaid
text:
# 10 Days Northeast India Tour

*event* November - March  
*schedule* 10 days

* [Classic](https://ceysaid.com/tour-tag/classic/)
* [Free and Easy](https://ceysaid.com/tour-tag/free-and-easy/)

## 10 Days Northeast India Tour

**SEND US A WHATSAPP MESSAGE FOR BOOKING YOUR TOUR**

**DAY 1 BAGDOGRA – DARJEELING (93 KMS 3.5 HOURS APPROX)**

Assistance on arrival at Bagdogra Airport and drive to Darjeeling.

***DARJEELING***

Standing high in the Himalayas at an altitude of 2134m, ‘Dorje Ling’, or place of the Thunderbolt, offers breathtaking views of snow-capped mountain peaks, with the Kanchendzonga rising higher than all the others. Often referred to as the “Queen of the Hills”, it remains just as alluring with its tiny waterfalls, little villages and a narrow gauge railway track. Surrounded by World Famous Tea Gardens and snowcapped mountains, Darjeeling remains one of the most exotic destinations. Graeme Westlake in his book ‘An introduction to hill stations on India’ refers to Darjeeling as having “A view Scarcely Unrivalled on Earth”.

Evening walk to Local market

Overnight at hotel.

**DAY 2 DARJEELING**

Early morning (04:00HRS) visit Tiger Hill for sunrise and back.

***TIGER HILL***

It is 13 kms from Darjeeling and one can see beautiful sunrise and Himalayan Range like Kanchandzonga , Mount Everest etc. One can see the horizon changing colours just before sunrise and then the entire Himalayan range turn golden. It is truly a majestic sight.

Breakfast at the hotel and go for Enjoy toy train ride up to Ghoom then visit Ghoom Monastery & back by Innova(Subject to operation of this train & availability of the seats) TOY TRAIN : The spectacular landscape unfolds as the 2-foot gauge Darjeeling Himalayan railway, opened in 1881, labours at about 10 mph criss-crossing the road. It has tiny 4-wheel locomotives (some over 100 years old) like living legends. One can experience the sound, smell and romance of a bygone era. DHR is a work of genius and technological achievement of 1881. It has social and cultural importance. It is beautiful and has outstanding universal appeal. Based on its unique features, it is considered to be of lasting significance to mankind.

***GHOOM MONASTERY***

This is oldest Monastery in the area which was built in 1875 and one can see the image of Mythey Buddha 15ft. height on the way back from Tiger Hill Afternoon proceed for city tour.

***HIMALAYAN MOUNTAINEERING INSTITUTE/PADMAJA NAIDU HIMALAYAN ZOOLOGICAL PARK (CLOSED ON THURSDAYS)***

The Himalayan Mountaineering Institute was started in Darjeeling when Tenzing Norgay climbed Everest to give training to learn Mountaineering and other adventure sports related to mountaineering and in that Institute one can see equipment etc.used by Mr. Tenzing Norgay and Everest Museum having complete details on Everest attached to institute one can visit Himalayan Zoological Park where one can see Himalayan animals like Snow Leopard, Tibetan Wolf, Tibetan Yak, Himalayan Black Bear and several varieties of birds.

***HAPPY VALLEY TEA ESTATE:***

It is the second oldest tea estate of Darjeeling (after Steinthal Tea Estate, which was established in 1852) and at a height of 2,100 metres (6,900 ft), is also one of the highest tea factories in the world. David Wilson an Englishman had named the garden Wilson Tea Estate and by 1860 had started cultivation of tea. In 1903, the estate was taken over by an Indian, Tarapada Banerjee, an aristocrat from Hooghly.

In 1929, Banerjee bought the Windsor Tea Estate nearby and merged the two estates under the name of Happy Valley Tea Estate. G.C. Banerjee was the next owner of Happy Valley Tea Estate. He with his wife Annapurna Devi and three daughters (Nonimukhi, Monmaya and Savitri) lived there for some time. Annapurna Devi was related to the Ganguly family of Khandwa her maternal uncle was Kunjalal Bihari, father of the famous cine Gangulys. Nandini Balial (Ganguly), a young prolific writer in LA is  
the great granddaughter of Late Monmaya Debi.

In March 2007, after remaining nearly dormant for nearly four years as the tea industry had experienced a slump, the estate was bought over by S K Bansal, of Ambootia Tea Group, which established a new factory within the premises, and started modernization process, replating and switching to organic farming. Finally, the estate reopened to public in 2008, with the original factory turned into a working museum. It also displayed single piston slow-speed engines, and the shaft machines and sells tea-related mementos.

title: 10 Days Northeast India Tour Package- Ceysaid
text:
Today over 1500 people worked in the tea estate and processing unit. In 2008, the hand-rolled tea produced by Happy Valley was chosen to be sold at Harrods in the United Kingdom, with price ranging from ₹5,000 (US$66) to ₹6,000 (US$80) per kg, besides this, it is also available at Mariage Freres in France.

***REMAINS CLOSED:***

(Sunday is the weekly holidays as such on Monday at Tea Estate/Garden there is no manufacture at the factory & during winter i.e., middle of Nov till Mar at Tea Estate there is no plucking or manufacture at the factory, one can just see the tea plantation only during this period. During festival period i.e., Durga Puja/Diwali festival tea garden will remain closed for 7-8 days). One can visit Tea Garden only.

***TIBETAN SELF- HELF CENTRE OR REFUGEE CENTRE (CLOSED ON SUNDAYS)***

This institute is run by Tibetan Refugees to preserve their handicrafts and one can see making of Tibetan Carpets, Leather work,  
Paintings and Wooden work etc. Overnight at hotel.

**DAY 3 DARJEELING – PELLING (110 KMS 5 HOURS APPROX.)**

Breakfast at the hotel and drive to Pelling.

***PELLING***

Located at a distance of 120 Kms from Gangtok Pelling is another serene, mountain village which lies in the western districts of Sikkim. This village is situated at an altitude of 2000 mts (approx.) and the distance from this village to the mount Kanchendzonga, as a crow flies…is 26 Kms approx. Afternoon Proceed for Sightseeing.

***PEMEYANGSTE MONASTERY***

2.5 Kms. from Pelling and standing at 2085m. The Perfect Sublime Lotus, perched on a wooded hilltop, commands the most sublime view of the Kanchendzonga.Built during the late 17th century, it is one of the oldest and most important monasteries of the Nyingmapa order of Buddhism in Sikkim. To the present day, it is only the monks of this monastery who enjoy the title of ‘Tasang’ or pure monks. The importance of this monastery grew with time and reached its peak when only monks from this monastery could anoint the reigning sovereign of the land with holy water.

Pemayangtse monastery follows the Mindroling tradition besides holding the lineage of Lhatsun Chenpo. Mindroling monastery was founded by Minling Terchen Gyurme Dorje whose famous daughter Jetsun Migyur Paldon lived near Pemayangtse and taught there during her exile. Her stone throne is still to be seen in this monastery, which has 108 monks. The main attraction of the monastery is the wooden replica of Zangdog Palri; the celestial abode of Guru Padmasambhava built by Serdup Lhundrup Dorje Rinpoche in 1971. The annual chaam (monk dance) is held here on the 28th and 29th day of the 12th month of the Tibetan lunar calendar.

***RABDENTSE***

Sikkim’s second capital. The ruins of Rabdantse are worth exploring. The winding track through the forest brings you to a small set of ruins of the early town and eventually on the ruins of the palace with Chorten (stupa) nearby. Farther afield are the Changay Falls. Overnight at hotel.

**DAY 4 PELLING**

After Breakfast visit to Sangachoeling Monastery, Chenrezig Statue, Sky walk, Khecheopalri lake and Rimbi Waterfalls.

***SANGACHOELING MONASTERY***

Located at the top of a ridge in the town of Pelling, Sikkim, Sangachoeling Monastery was established in the 17th century by Lama Gyalwa Lhatsun Chempo. The literal meaning of the word Sanga Choeling is ‘Idea of Guhya Mantra Teachings’ that relates to the faith of Buddhism. Also spelt as Sange Choeling Monastery, this is one of the oldest monasteries in Northeastern Sikkim. In order to reach this place, one has to walk for at least 40 minutes through the forest on a steep hill slope but the end result is absolutely worth it. Stepping inside the Sangachoeling Monastery, you notice various ancient wall paintings, statues, unique clay structures and scriptures that date back to the 17th century. They are extremely beautiful and are well- preserved.

From Sangachoeling Monastery one also gets an opportunity to witness a spectacular view of the snow clad mountains and lush greenery around it, providing a feeling of calmness. The fact that the building was restored to its former beauty after catching fire has become the major highlight of the monastery. Due to this reason, people come from nook and corner of the world visit the prestigious  
Sangachoeling Monastery building to have a close look at the ancient artefacts.

***KHECHEOPALRI LAKE***

It is a Holy Lake for Buddhists in West Sikkim, in Sikkim, the tiny Himalayan former Buddhist Kingdom in eastern India, now and a part of the democratic republic. Khecheopalri is also considered as a wish fulfilling lake and has thousands of non-Buddhist believers who pray here.

title: 10 Days Northeast India Tour Package- Ceysaid
text:
The real name of the lake is Sho Dzo Sho, meaning “Oh Lady, Sit Here”, but is commonly known as Khachoedpaldri lake because it sits on the lap of Khachoedpaldri, a very sacred hill. The origin of the lake as narrated in the sacred scriptures of Sikkimese Buddhism starts after the Nepali invasion of the medieval kingdom in the 1780s. The Nepali army destroyed the original (third) palace of the Namgyal Dynasty situated at Rabdentse.

***THE CHENREZIG STATUE***

It is one of the world’s tallest statues of Buddha which is considered to be a manifestation of Buddha’s compassion. It is located about 1.7 km away from Pelling skywalk and stands at a height of a whopping 135 feet. The statue is a major tourist hub and one of great cultural significance amongst Budhists, who visit the site seeking Buddha’s blessings. It was consecrated by the Dalai Lama himself and recently unveiled by Pawan Kumar Chamling, the chief minister of Sikkim. The statue can be reached via stairs and once atop, one can feast their eyes on the scenic views of the valley. The meditative chants of the monks, the bustling skywalk and the serene weather makes for a blissful experience.

***SKYWALK***

The Glass Skywalk is India’s first ever skywalk, set in the picturesque town of Pelling in Northeast Sikkim. Built at an astounding height of 7200 feet, this colossal structure allows tourists to look down several thousand feet in the air, from the peak to the very bottom of mountains. The Glass Skywalk is not for the fainthearted, or for people with vertigo as walking on transparent glass at such heights can be spine-chilling!. The Pelling skywalk is a transparent foot-over bridge made out of glass that is suspended hundreds of feet in the air. Since its inauguration in November 2018, the skywalk has become a major hub of tourists owing to the odd concoction of thrill and peace that it has to offer. There are several attractions that can be viewed from over the bridge including waterfalls, lush forests and monasteries. It is located adjacent to the marvelous statue of Chenrezig, which is a famous Buddhist pilgrimage site. The skywalk overlooks the magnificent Kanchenjunga, as well as the Teesta and Rangit rivers, which flow alongside adding another dimension to the serenity of the place.

***THE RIMBI WATERFALLS***

It is one of the tourist attractions of Sikkim which is located about 12kms from Pelling at about 5kms from the Darap Village, another attraction of Sikkim. This waterfall can be seen throughout the year although in the rainy season it looks much more beautiful. The waterfall leads to Rimbi River and is famous for the recreational activities like fishing. The Karchen Powerhouse is the oldest powerhouse in west Sikkim. The powerhouse was built in the early 70s by the last king’ government. It used to supply water to all the neighbouring villages during that period. Overnight at hotel.

***DAY 5 PELLING – GANGTOK VIA RAVANGLA (125 KMS 05HRS APPROX)***

After breakfast drive to Gangtok via Ravangla

***THE BUDDHA PARK OF RAVANGLA***

It is also known as Tathagata Tsal, is situated near Rabong (Ravangla) in South Sikkim district, Sikkim, India. It was constructed between 2006 and 2013 and features a 130-foot high statue of the Buddha as its centerpiece. The site was chosen within the larger religious complex of the Rabong Gompa (Monastery), itself a centuries-old place of pilgrimage. Also nearby is Ralang Monastery, a key monastery in Tibetan Buddhism.The statue was consecrated on 25 March 2013 by the 14th Dalai Lama, and became a stop on the 'Himalayan Buddhist Circuit'.

The statue of the Buddha marks the occasion of the 2550th birth anniversary of Gautama Buddha. This statue was built and installed in the place through the joint efforts of the Sikkim government and its people. The Buddhist circuit of this park was built here under a State government project, for boosting pilgrimage and tourism in the region. Then drive to Gangtok.

***GANGTOK***

At an altitude of 1750m, Gangtok became the state capital in the mid 19th century and has undergone rapid development since then.The downtown area is dominated by huge government buildings decorated with traditional Buddhist symbols. Gangtok is also a resting ground for people traveling to North Sikkim. Surrounded by Monasteries and Orchids, Gangtok is truly a travellers delight.  
Overnight at hotel.

**DAY 6 GANGTOK – LACHUNG (117 KMS 6.5 HOURS APPROX.)**

Early morning breakfast at the hotel in Gangtok and drive to Lachung and on the way visit Chungthang

***LACHUNG***

title: 10 Days Northeast India Tour Package- Ceysaid
text:
**DAY 6 GANGTOK – LACHUNG (117 KMS 6.5 HOURS APPROX.)**

Early morning breakfast at the hotel in Gangtok and drive to Lachung and on the way visit Chungthang

***LACHUNG***

Lachung is a town and hill station in northeast Sikkim, India. It is located in the North Sikkim district near the border with Tibet. Lachung is at an elevation of about 9,600 feet (2,900 m) and at the confluence of the lachen and Lachung Rivers, both tributaries of the River Teesta. The word Lachung means "small pass". The town is approximately 125 kilometres (78 mi) from the capital Gangtok. The town's economy has been boosted by tourism in recent years as the region has been opened up by the Indian government.

Tourists come from all over the world to visit the town between October and May, mostly on their way to the Yumthang Valley and the Lachung Monastery. Most of Lachung's inhabitants are of Lepcha and Tibetan descent. Languages spoken here are Nepali, Lepcha and Bhutia. During winter the town is usually covered in snow. Overnight at hotel.

**DAY 7 LACHUNG – YUMTHANG VALLEY – LACHUNG**

Morning proceed for excursion to Yumthang Valley.

***YUMTHANG VALLEY***

The extremely picturesque Yumthang Valley is situated at an elevation of 3564 m in the North of Sikkim. The place is amongst the most famous tourist destinations in Sikkim, popularly known as the ‘valley of flowers’ because of its unique location above the line where no trees grow. The shrubby slopes of the snow-clad mountains around the valley, the long stretches of land covered with flowery meadows and the scented environment makes this place a nature's wonderland that attracts many tourists from around the world.

The valley is the vantage point for the beautiful peaks of Panhunri and Shundu Tsenpa forming an amazing scenic setting for the gushing Teesta river. The grazing yaks amidst thousands of blooming Himalayan flowers and an astounding collection of Rhododendrons entice the visitors to stay for a little more time.

***HOT SPRING***

It is believed that due to high in Sulphur content these hot springs have medicinal value and therefore, they are ideal for taking a dip. The Yumthang hot water spring is on the way to Yumthang valley, also popularly known as the Valley of Flowers across the Lachung chu river. The water is heated due to the presence of sulphur which is then mixed with the icy cold water of Lachung chu river. The heated water is channelized in Log huts, a dip in warm water is quite refreshing. To reach the spot from the main road one needs to walk for a few hundred yards. Overnight at hotel.

**DAY 8 LACHUNG – GANGTOK**  
After breakfast drive from Lachung to Gangtok. En-route visit Lachung Monastery.

***LACHUNG MONASTERY***

Lachung Monastery is located amid apple orchards just across the scenic Lachung Chu river. It lies at a height of about 2,750 m and forms the base of the Green Lake trek. A symbol of cultural and religious heritage, Lachung Monastery has an edifice that was installed in 1806 by the Nyingmapa sect of Buddhism.

It also houses a statue of Guru Padmasambhava, who is believed to be one of the founding fathers of Tibetan Buddhism. The monastery is a small and colourful structure that has a two-storey prayer hall. On the porch lie two extra-heavy prayer wheels, along with two metal dragons that are carved above it. Overnight at hotel.

**DAY 9 GANGTOK – TSOMGO LAKE - GANGTOK**

After Breakfast excursion to Tsomgo Lake & back.

***TSOMGO LAKE***

35 Kms from Gangtok at an altitude of 12,400 feet is the beautiful Tsomgo Lake, which freezes over in winter. The lake derives its waters from melting snows off the surrounding mountains. Legends say that lamas could forecast the future by studying the color of the lake’s waters. Fishing is totally prohibited and all are requested not to dirty the environs of the lake to maintain its sanctity. Overnight at hotel.

**DAY 10 GANGTOK – BAGDOGRA AIRPORT**

Transfer to Bagdogra airport for your onward journey

title: 06 Days Kashmir Tour Package - Ceysaid
text:
# 06 Days Kashmir Tour Package

*event* November - March  
*schedule* 6 days

* [Classic](https://ceysaid.com/tour-tag/classic/)
* [Free and Easy](https://ceysaid.com/tour-tag/free-and-easy/)

## 06 Days Kashmir Tour Package

**SEND US A WHATSAPP MESSAGE FOR BOOKING YOUR TOUR**

**DAY 01 Colombo – Delhi (by Flight) Delhi – Srinagar (by Domestic Flight) Dinner**

On arrival at Srinagar, you will be met by our representative and transfer you to Houseboat. Afternoon visit to Shankarcharaya Temple where from you will have the fantastic view of the whole Srinagar city and lakes.

Later tour of world famous Mughal Gardens visiting the Nishat Bagh (The garden of pleasure), Shalimar Bagh (Abode of love) and Botnical Garden. In the evening Shikara ride on The Dal Lake to enjoy the beauty that God has graced this city with which is often compared to Venice. You will get a view of some very interesting places around the Lake. Overnight at Srinagar.

**DAY 02 Srinagar – Sonmarg – Srinagar 168 Kms Breakfast / Dinner**

Full day excursion to Sonmarg which is the most beautiful drive from Srinagar. On the way we stop at many beautiful spots. Continue drive to Sonmarg 2690 Mtrs (Meadow of Gold). One can ride on horse to visit to Thajiwas Glacier where snow remains round the year and Sonmarg is known as Gateway of Ladakh. In the evening return to Srinagar. Overnight at Srinagar. .

**DAY 03 Srinagar – Pahalgam 96 Kms Breakfast / Dinner**

After breakfast, leave from Srinagar and drive to Pahalgam 2440 Mtrs (Vale of Kashmir) on the way visit Saffron fields and Avantipur ruins which is eleven hundred years old temple. Finally by the lunch time you will reach Pahalgam which is the most famous place for Indian Film Industry. Afternoon enjoy the nature charm of the valley. Overnight at Hotel.

**DAY 04 Pahalgam Breakfast / Dinner**

Day to enjoy the beauty of the Pahalgam valley and explore Pahalgam on your own. Sightseeing of Aru, Chandanwari and Bhetab valley (using local union time bound vehicle) Chandanwari at 2,895 m and 16 km (10 miles) from Pahalgam, is the starting point of the Amamathyatra. The road from Pahalgam to Chandanwari is curvy, sometimes a relatively flat terrain, with quite a few steep curves.

It can be undertaken by car (not busses and trucks for most part) with some skilled driving. From Chandanwari onwards the track becomes much steeper, being accessible on foot or by pony only. 11 km from Chandanwari is the mountain lake of Sheshnag, after which, 13 km away is the last stop, Panchtarni. A narrow spiralling path from Panchtarni leads to the Amarnath Cave. ARU A small village, 11 kms upstream the Lidder River, is a beautiful locality. The route is picturesque with ample campsites. This village is the starting point for treks to the Kolahoiglacier. This charming meadow can be reached by walking along a mountain path. The river Lidder disappears at GurKhumb and reappears after 27 mts.

Betaab Valley, situated just 7 km up on Pahalgam, has got its name from Sunny Deol’s movie, Betaab, which was shot extensively in this area. Surrounded by snow-covered mountain peaks and dense pine and deodar forests, this valley is the mouth of River Sheshnag. There is a beautiful garden in the valley, with a bridge to cross the river. The Pahalgam Development Authority has proposed to develop this valley as a beautiful holidaying spot and is thus, carrying out several construction activities in the region.  
Afternoon visit to Poshwan Park, Deer Park and Amusment park Overnight at the hotel in Pahalgam.

**DAY 05 Pahalgam– Gulmarg 149 Kms. Breakfast / Dinner**

After breakfast leave for Gulmarg which is one of the most beautiful summer resort in the valley. It is also popular for its golf course (in summer), the highest in the world and skiing during the winter season. Enjoy the splendor of nature and the snowy mountains. One can enjoy Gondola ride (cable car please book online at www.jammukashmircablecar.com) from Gulmarg to Kongdori 1st Phase The Phase I of the Gulmarg gondola takes visitors from the ski resort to the bowl-shaped Kongdori Valley (10,100 feet ) and second phase of the cable car ride takes you from Kongdori Station to Aparwath Mountain (13,057 feet). Overnight at Gulmarg.

**DAY 06 Gulmarg - Srinagar 56 Kms Srinagar – Delhi (by Domestic Flight) Delhi – Colombo (by Flight) Breakfast**

Transfer in time to Srinagar airport to connect flight for onwards destination

**SUPPLEMENTS**  
1. Supplement of Gandola (Cable Car) Ride 1st Phase @ USD 15 Per Person  
2. Supplement of Gandola (Cable Car) Ride 2nd Phase @ USD 30 Per Person

title: 05 Days Tibet Tour Package - Ceysaid
text:
# 05 Days Tibet Tour Package

*event* November - March  
*schedule* 5 days

* [Classic](https://ceysaid.com/tour-tag/classic/)
* [Free and Easy](https://ceysaid.com/tour-tag/free-and-easy/)

## 05 Days Tibet Tour Package

**SEND US A WHATSAPP MESSAGE FOR BOOKING YOUR TOUR**

**Day 1: Arrive in Lhasa by plane or by train.**

Lhasa train station/ airport picking up. You will be warmly greeted by our local guide and  
driver, transfer you to reserved hotel in Lhasa. Today is your first day in Lhasa, so have a good rest to acclimatize the high altitude. Overnight in Lhasa.

**Day 2: Lhasa Sightseeing: Potala Palace/Jokhang Temple/Barkhor/ StreetSera Monastery.**

In the morning we arranged you to visit the Potala Palace which is the worldwide known cardinal landmark of Tibet. The massive structure itself contains a small world within it. Mostly it is renowned as residence of the Dalai Lama lineages (Avalokiteshvara). Inside Potala palace, you will see the throne room of Dalai Lamas and their golden funeral stupa. Afternoon section is to visit the most sacred shrine and spiritual heart of Tibet, the Johkang Temple, which is built in 7th century by 33RD King Songtsen Gampo. Barkhor street, most known ritual circumambulation where you can see Tibetan locals prostrating and murmuring mantras in strong faith.

After you can ask your guide to take you to Local tea stall to have some sweet milk tea .visit the Sera Monastery, which was founded in 1419 by Tsongkapa's disciple Jamchen Choeje. The most attractive is Monks debating which is related to logic,Overnight at Lhasa.

**Day 03: Lhasa/Yamdrotso lake/Gyangtse/Shigatse**

Driving over Kambala pass 4794m, you will have a glimpse of Yamdrok-tso and spectacular views of Holy Mount Nyenchen Khangsar in the distance, the turquoise colored Lake Yamdrok Yutso is one of the three holy lakes in Tibet, the unforgettable vista of pincer-shaped turquoise water of the lake and amazing spectacle of Mt. Nyenchen Khangsar glacier from Karo la pass are wonders of the day. Then along the Friendship southern Hwy drive to Gyantse, visit the famous stupa - Gyantse Kumbum, one of the most stunning architectural wonders in Tibet. Drive to Shigatse. Overnight in Shigatse

**Day 4: Shigatse- Lhasa**

Tibet’s second largest city. Shigatse, elevation 3840m, is home to the Tashilhunpo Monastery. Along with exploring the monastery, you can also hike the ancient pilgrim circuit along with hundreds of Tibetan worshippers. Drive through Northern friendship highway to Lhasa near the Yarlung Tsangpo river.

**Day5: Lhasa airport dropping off**  
The driver will take you to airport or train station.

title: Istanbul Cappadocia Tour Package - Ceysaid
text:
# Istanbul Cappadocia Tour

*event* November - March  
*schedule* 6 days

* [Classic](https://ceysaid.com/tour-tag/classic/)
* [Free and Easy](https://ceysaid.com/tour-tag/free-and-easy/)

## Istanbul Cappadocia Tour

**SEND US A WHATSAPP MESSAGE FOR BOOKING YOUR TOUR**

**DAY 1) ISTANBUL – ARRIVAL**  
Arrive in Istanbul NEW Airport (flight details to be advised) you will meet with English speaking representative holding a name sign outside the luggage claim area at the airport, transfer to hotel by a private vehicle for check-in & rest. (Early check-in upon availability) Remainder of the day at your own leisure.  
Overnight at hotel in Istanbul.

**DAY 2) ISTANBUL FULL DAY OLD CITY TOUR** *(Buffet Breakfast & Local Lunch included)*  
After your breakfast meet with your English speaking professional guide at the hotel lobby for your REGULAR (SIC) Istanbul Old City tour. First you will visit the magnificent TOPKAPI PALACE, the residence of the sultans for almost three centuries. The construction of the Topkapı Palace was completed between 1465 and 1478. Being the imperial residence of the sultan, his court and harem, the palace was also the seat of government for the Ottoman Empire.

In the Treasury section, you will see breathtaking displays, including the 7th largest diamond in the world, the “Spoonmaker’s Diamond”. At the chamber of Holy Mantle you can see the sacred leavings from Prophet Mohammad (PBU). (Entrance fee for Harem section is not included.)

Next you will visit the HIPPODROME, which was one of the largest chariot race grounds of the Byzantine Empire. Here you can see the Egyptian Obelisk brought from Egypt by Emperor Theodosius; the Serpentine Column (Tripod of Plataea) from Temple of Apollo at Delphi and the Tiled (Walled) Column erected by Constantine Porphyrogenitus at 10th century AD. Then you will visit the serene BLUE MOSQUE one of Istanbul’s most valuable monuments. It was built in 7 years (1606-1609 AD) with the order of Ottoman Sultan Ahmed I. From the courtyard you will enjoy the striking view of the cascading domes and semi-domes. Once inside you will see how the mosque got its name, viewing the colorful blue Iznik tiles that adorn the interior. These tiles were made at the peak of Iznik tile production in the early 17th Century. Lunch will be served at a Local Restaurant.

After lunch you will visit the ST. SOPHIA (from outside), the church of the Divine Wisdom. The architectural marvel of all time. It was built during Emperor Justinian reign (AD537) and it remained the greatest church in Christendom until the conquest of Constantinople in 1453 by Sultan Mehmet II who ordered to convert Hagia Sophia into a Mosque. In 1934, proclaimed as a museum.  
After St. Sophia, you will visit the GRAND BAZAAR, the largest and oldest covered Bazaar in Turkey with more than 4,000 shops. A variety of handicrafts, carpets, rare jewels and souvenirs all sold in small shops next to each other. The merchants are selling everything from antiques, home wares, books, copper ware, ceramics, gold and souvenirs. Venture into the hidden courtyards or hans, lookout for marble fountains and ornate kiosks. Streets are named according to the trades and the most oriental atmosphere in Istanbul is found in the bazaar. At the end of your tour, back to your hotel. Dinner on your own. Overnight at hotel in Istanbul.

**DAY 3) ISTANBUL** *(Buffet Breakfast included)*  
Breakfast at hotel Free day Oevrnight at hotel.

**DAY 4) Istanbul - Cappadocia** *(Buffet Breakfast included)*  
Check out from your hotel, private vehicle and driver will take you to the airport for a quick, direct flight from Istanbul to Kayseri or Nevsehir Airport. Upon arrival, you will be transferred to your hotel and enjoy free time for the rest of the day.

**DAY 5) Highlights of Cappadocia** *(Buffet Breakfast & Local Lunch included)*

(It is possible on this day to book an optional, early morning hot air balloon tour to enjoy the magnificent view of the valleys from the air.)

Meet with your guide at hotel lobby and depart from hotel to have full day regular (SIC) Cappadocia tour. First visit Devrent Valley to see the unique fairy-chimneys. Drive to Paşabağ (Monk’s Valley) to see the most interesting mushroom-shaped pinnacles. Drive to the city of Avanos. Lunch at local restaurant as set menu (drinks extra). In the afternoon visit another beautiful open air museum at Göreme where the remains of rock-cut churches and Christian settlements date back hundreds of years. Lastly, before returning to your hotel, visit the natural castles of Uçhisar. After tour back to hotel.

title: Istanbul Kusadas Tour Package - Ceysaid
text:
# Istanbul Kusadas Tour Package

*event* November - March  
*schedule* 6 days

* [Classic](https://ceysaid.com/tour-tag/classic/)
* [Free and Easy](https://ceysaid.com/tour-tag/free-and-easy/)

## Istanbul Kusadas Tour Package

SEND US A WHATSAPP MESSAGE FOR BOOKING YOUR TOUR

**DAY 1) ISTANBUL – ARRIVAL** *(No Meals)*  
Arrive in Istanbul NEW Airport (flight details to be advised) you will meet with English speaking Travel Ideas representative holding a name sign outside the luggage claim area at the airport, transfer to hotel by a private vehicle for check-in & rest. (Early check-in upon availability) Remainder of the day at your own leisure. Overnight at hotel in Istanbul.

**DAY 2) ISTANBUL FULL DAY OLD CITY TOUR** *(Buffet Breakfast & Local Lunch included)*  
After your breakfast meet with your English speaking professional guide at the hotel lobby for your REGULAR (SIC) Istanbul Old City tour. First you will visit the magnificent TOPKAPI PALACE, the residence of the sultans for almost three centuries. The construction of the Topkapı Palace was completed between 1465 and 1478. Being the imperial residence of the sultan, his court and harem, the palace was also the seat of government for the Ottoman Empire.

In the Treasury section, you will see breathtaking displays, including the 7th largest diamond in the world, the “Spoonmaker’s Diamond”. At the chamber of Holy Mantle you can see the sacred leavings from Prophet Mohammad (PBU). (Entrance fee for Harem section is not included.)

Next you will visit the HIPPODROME, which was one of the largest chariot race grounds of the Byzantine Empire. Here you can see the Egyptian Obelisk brought from Egypt by Emperor Theodosius; the Serpentine Column (Tripod of Plataea) from Temple of Apollo at Delphi and the Tiled (Walled) Column erected by Constantine Porphyrogenitus at 10th century AD. Then you will visit the serene BLUE MOSQUE one of Istanbul’s most valuable monuments. It was built in 7 years (1606-1609 AD) with the order of Ottoman Sultan  
Ahmed I. From the courtyard you will enjoy the striking view of the cascading domes and semi-domes. Once inside you will see how the mosque got its name, viewing the colorful blue Iznik tiles that adorn the interior. These tiles were made at the peak of Iznik tile production in the early 17th Century. Lunch will be served at a Local Restaurant.

After lunch you will visit the ST. SOPHIA (from outside), the church of the Divine Wisdom. The architectural marvel of all time. It was built during Emperor Justinian reign (AD537) and it remained the greatest church in Christendom until the conquest of Constantinople in 1453 by Sultan Mehmet II who ordered to convert Hagia Sophia into a Mosque. In 1934, proclaimed as a museum.  
After St. Sophia, you will visit the GRAND BAZAAR, the largest and oldest covered Bazaar in Turkey with more than 4,000 shops. A variety of handicrafts, carpets, rare jewels and souvenirs all sold in small shops next to each other.

The merchants are selling everything from antiques, home wares, books, copper ware, ceramics, gold and souvenirs. Venture into the hidden courtyards or hans, lookout for marble fountains and ornate kiosks. Streets are named according to the trades and the most oriental atmosphere in Istanbul is found in the bazaar. At the end of your tour, back to your hotel. Dinner on your own. Overnight at hotel in Istanbul.

**DAY 3) ISTANBUL - KUSADASI** *(Breakfast)*  
Breakfast at hotel, check out and transfer to Istanbul or Sabiha Gokcen airport to take a flight to Izmir. Upon arrival to Izmir airport, private airport transfer to your hotel at Kusadasi. Free time. Dinner on your own. Overnight at hotel in Kusadasi.

**DAY 4) EPHESUS TOUR** *(Breakfast & Local Lunch)*  
After breakfast, you will be escorted for your full day Ephesus city tour. The tour starting with Isa Bey Mosque, then drive to an ancient Greek city, later a major Roman city, on the west coast of Asia Minor which called Ephesus, stroll past the celsus library, the Temple Of Hadrian and the Trajan Fountain in the Ancient Ephesus. After walking on the historical marble street the Great Theater where St. Paul preached will meet at the end. Open buffed lunch in a local Turkish restaurant.

In the afternoon will visit the carpet cooperative to see how the Turkish carpets are made by hand. Proceed to shrine of Mother Mary, it is believed where she spent recent years of her life. Temple Of Artemis (Diana), on the side of one of the seven wonders of ancient World. At the end Leather Fashion show will be full of fun. Lunch at local restaurant. At the end of your tour, back to your hotel. Dinner on your own. Overnight at hotel in Kusadasi.

title: Istanbul Kusadas Tour Package - Ceysaid
text:
**DAY 5) PAMUKKALE TOU** *(Breakfast & Local Lunch)*  
Breakfast at hotel. Today you will discover Hierapolis. Drive to Pamukkale that has the meaning of the Cotton Castle in English. Once known as Hierapolis, famous for healing thermal / mineral Baths and beautiful snow-white “petrified waterfalls. After lunch visit Hierapolis, including the magnificently preserved Necropolis, the main Street and Gates, the thermal Baths, the devil’s hole, and Masterpiece Theater. Pamukkale terraces are made of travertine, a sedimentary rock deposited by water from the hot springs. In this area, there are 17 hot water springs in which the temperature ranges from 35 °C to 100 °C The water that emerges from the spring is transported 320 meters (1,050 ft.) to the head of the travertine terraces and deposits calcium carbonate on a section 60 to 70 meters long covering an expanse of 240 meters to 300 meters. Swimming or a therapeutic bath in a hotel pool’s hot spring mineral Waters. At the end of your tour, back to your hotel. Dinner on your own. Overnight at hotel in Kusadasi.

**DAY 6) KUSADASI – ISTANBUL DEPARTURE** *(Breakfast Included)*  
Breakfast at hotel, check out and transfer to Izmir airport to take a flight to Istanbul. Upon arrival to Istanbul airport for your fly back for your International departure flight at the airport. END OF OUR SERVICES…

title: Turkey Visa - Ceysaid
text:
# Turkey Visa

## **Required Documents for Turkey Visa Application**

Applicants must submit the following documents when applying for a Turkey visa:

1. **Passport & Copies**
   * A valid passport with at least **six months of validity** beyond the intended stay.
   * A photocopy of the passport’s biographical page.
   * All previous passports (if applicable).
   * If a passport is lost, a **police report translated into English** must be submitted.
2. **Visa Application Form**
   * A **duly filled** and signed visa application form.
3. **Photographs**
   * **Two recent biometric photographs** (5×6 cm) with a **white background**.
   * The uploaded and submitted photographs must be the **same**.
4. **Invitation Letter (Optional)**
   * If applicable, an invitation letter from Türkiye.
   * The invitation letter should be attested by a **notary public in Türkiye**.
5. **Financial & Employment Documents**
   * Documents proving the financial status of both the applicant and the inviting person.
   * **Official letter from employer/school/university** confirming leave approval.
   * A **signed letter from the applicant** explaining the purpose of the visit.
   * **Salary slips or confirmation letter of salary** for the past three months.
   * **Bank statements for the last six months** showing financial stability.
6. **Travel & Accommodation Details**
   * **Flight reservation** (round-trip).
   * **Hotel booking or invitation letter** from a host in Türkiye.
   * The invitation letter should be **notarized** in Türkiye.
7. **Additional Supporting Documents**
   * Proof of **commitment to return**, such as a **land registry, business certificate, salary table, or bank account statement**.
   * A **No Objection Letter (NOC) or affidavit** from the non-accompanying parent(s) for children under 18, attested by the **Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Sri Lanka**.
   * **Marriage certificate and birth certificates of children** for family applications.

## **Processing Time & Fees**

* The visa processing time ranges from **15 days up to 2 months**, depending on the application and embassy approval.
* The visa **fee is non-refundable** under any circumstances.

## **Visa Requirements for Sri Lankan Nationals**

* **Official and ordinary passport holders** are required to obtain a visa before entering Türkiye.
* **Sri Lankan nationals** holding a valid **Schengen, USA, UK, or Ireland visa or residence permit** may be eligible for an **e-Visa**.

For any assistance or inquiries, feel free to contact our visa support team. Safe travels!

title: Thailand Visa - Ceysaid
text:
# Thailand Visa

# **Thailand Visa for Sri Lankan Nationals**

## **Visa Options**

![✅](https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/2705.svg) **Visa on Arrival (VOA) & E-Visa Available**  
Sri Lankan passport holders can apply for a **Visa on Arrival (VOA)** at designated entry points or apply for an **E-Visa** online before traveling to Thailand.

## **E-Visa Requirements**

To apply for a **Thailand E-Visa**, applicants must submit the following documents:

1. **Passport or Travel Document**
2. **Recent Photograph**
3. **Proof of Current Location**
4. **Contact Information**
5. **Travel & Accommodation Details**

## **Visa Types & Validity**

| **Visa Type** | **Number of Entries** | **Validity** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Single Entry Visa** | 1 Entry | Valid for **3 months** |
| **Multiple Entry Visa** | Multiple Entries | Valid for **6 months** |

## **Processing Time**

* The standard processing time for a **Thailand E-Visa** is **5 to 10 working days** after the submission of a complete application.
* Some applications may take longer depending on additional verification requirements by the Thai Embassy.

### **Important Notes:**

* The **visa fee is non-refundable** under any circumstances.
* Applicants must ensure all documents are accurate and complete to avoid delays.

For further assistance or inquiries, feel free to contact our visa support team. Safe travels!

title: Qatar Visa - Ceysaid
text:
# Qatar Visa

## **Required Documents for Qatar Visa Application**

Applicants must submit the following documents for a **Qatar tourist visa**:

1. **White Background Colored Scanned Photo**
   * A **high-quality scanned copy** of a recent **passport-sized color photo** with a white background.
   * **Mobile phone photos are NOT accepted** – only scanned copies will be processed.
2. **Passport Data Page**
   * A **colored scanned copy** of the passport’s **biographical page**.
   * The passport must be valid for **at least six months** from the travel date.
3. **Travel & Accommodation Details** *(Provided by Us)*
   * **Hotel voucher** (arranged by us).
   * **Airline ticket itinerary** (arranged by us).
4. **Visa Rejection History**
   * Applicants must provide information **if they have ever had a visa rejected** by any country.
5. **Family & Marital Status Documents**
   * **Married couples** must submit a **marriage certificate**.
   * **Families with children** must provide **birth certificates**.
   * **Single travelers** may face stricter visa approval conditions.
6. **Additional Documents**
   * **Qatar immigration may request additional documents**, and applicants will be informed accordingly.

## **Document Naming Format**

* All files must be **renamed with the applicant’s name** for faster processing.
* Example:
  + *John Passport, John Form 14, John Photo, John Ticket*

## **Visa Processing & Terms**

* **Processing Time:** **8-10 working days**
* **Visa Approval is NOT Guaranteed.**
* **No rejection reason or documents will be provided by Qatar immigration.**
* **Full package booking (hotel + flight) is required** once the visa is approved.
* **Visa fees are non-refundable** even if the visa is rejected.

![📌](https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f4cc.svg) **This visa is strictly for tourism purposes only.**

For any further inquiries, feel free to contact our visa support team. Safe travels!

title: Philippine Visa - Ceysaid
text:
# Philippine Visa

## **Required Documents for Philippines Visa Application**

Applicants must submit the following documents for a **Philippines visa**:

### **1. Visa Application Form**

* A **duly completed** visa application form.
* Must be filled out in **black or blue ink** using **BLOCK letters**.

### **2. Passport & Photocopies**

* A **passport valid for at least six (6) months** beyond the intended stay.
* **Photocopies** of the biometric page and any alteration pages.

### **3. Photographs**

* **Two recent colored photographs** (2″ x 2″) with a white background.

### **4. Proof of Financial Capacity**

**a. Bank Statements:**

* **Original bank statements** showing **substantial transactions for the last six (6) months**.
* **Certified by the bank** (E-statements must also be certified).
* If no statements are available, an **updated bank book (certified by the bank)** must be provided.
* Minimum balance requirement: **Rs. 300,000** or more, maintained consistently.
* Fixed deposits (certified by the bank) can be submitted as an optional document.
* **Consent letter for joint bank accounts** (if applicable).
* If **sponsored by an immediate family member**, proof of relationship is required (**birth or marriage certificate**, translated into English and certified by a notary public or JP).

**b. Business Income Proof (For Business Owners):**

* Copy of the **Business Registration Certificate**, certified by the **Divisional Secretariat**.

### **5. Travel & Accommodation Details**

* **Flight reservations** (entry & exit from the Philippines).
* **Hotel bookings or proof of lodging** for the duration of stay.

### **6. Sponsorship & Company Support (If Applicable)**

* **For incentive tours sponsored by a company:**
  + A sponsorship letter signed by **two directors OR one director & a senior manager**.
  + Certified copy of the **Business Registration Certificate**.
  + **List of directors**, certified by the **company secretary**.
  + If the applicant is a director, another director and a senior manager must sign.
* **If sponsored from the Philippines:**
  + **Letter of invitation**, authenticated by a **notary public in the Philippines**.
  + Sponsor’s **passport copy**.
  + Sponsor’s **certified bank statements for the last six (6) months**.

### **7. Employment & Ties to Home Country**

* **Letter from employer** granting leave and confirming return to Sri Lanka.
* If self-employed, a **personal letter explaining the trip**.
* Documents proving **ties to the home country**, such as:
  + **Employment contract or letter**.
  + **Property ownership documents** (if requested).
  + **Sri Lankan residence visa** (for foreign passport holders residing in Sri Lanka).

### **8. Police Clearance (If Requested)**

* The **Philippine Consulate may require a police clearance** at a later stage.

## **Processing Time & Visa Validity**

* **Processing Time:** **10-15 working days** (subject to DFA approval).
* **Single-entry visa** valid for **3 months**.

---

## **Additional Requirements for Minor Applicants (Sri Lankans)**

* **Birth certificate** (Sinhala & English translation) – *Original required at submission, or a certified copy by a JP/notary public*.
* **Marriage certificate of parents** (English translation).
* **Letter from parents** confirming that the child is accompanied by them, along with their passport copies.
* If parents are **not accompanying the child**, a **notarized consent letter** from both parents is required.
* **For students:** A letter from the **school/university** mentioning the passport number and granting permission to travel.

---

## **General Instructions for All Visa Applications**

![✔️](https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/2714.svg) All relevant letters must be addressed to the **Consulate General of the Republic of the Philippines, Colombo**, mentioning the correct address and the applicant’s passport details.

![✔️](https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/2714.svg) The **Consulate may request additional documents** at its discretion.

![✔️](https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/2714.svg) Submission of complete documents **does not guarantee visa approval**.

![✔️](https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/2714.svg) Applications missing any required documents **will not be processed**. The decision of the **Processing Officer is final**.

![✔️](https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/2714.svg) **Group Applications (5 or more applicants)** must be submitted **before 11:00 AM**.

![✔️](https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/2714.svg) **Visa Fee:** The applicable visa fee is payable in **Sri Lankan Rupees** and is **subject to change without notice**. **Visa fees are non-refundable**.

title: Myanmar Visa - Ceysaid
text:
# Myanmar Visa

### **Visa Requirements for Travelers**

To apply for a Myanmar visa, ensure you meet the following requirements:

1. **Passport Validity**
   * Your passport must be valid for **at least six (6) months** from the date of arrival in Myanmar.
2. **Photograph**
   * **One recent color photo** (4.6 cm x 3.8 cm) taken within the **last three (3) months**.
3. **Passport Bio-Data Page**
   * A **clear scanned copy** of your **passport bio-data page** must be uploaded.
4. **Proof of Return Ticket**
   * A confirmed **return ticket** from Myanmar must be attached to the application.
5. **Hotel Booking Confirmation**
   * Travelers must provide **proof of hotel booking** for their stay.
   * **Tourists must stay in registered hotels, motels, inns, guesthouses, or resorts** during their visit.

### **Processing Time**

* The minimum processing time for a **Tourist e-Visa (T)** is **three (3) working days** (Myanmar time).

### **Entry Points for eVisa Holders**

Currently, passengers with an **approved eVisa** are permitted to enter Myanmar **only through** the following international entry points:

![✈️](https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/2708.svg) **Airports:**

* Yangon International Airport
* Mandalay International Airport
* Nay Pyi Taw International Airport

![🚪](https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f6aa.svg) **Land Border Checkpoint:**

* Kawthaung International Land Border Checkpoint

### **Important Notes**

* **Visa fees are non-refundable**, even if the application is rejected.
* Travelers should ensure they meet all entry requirements before applying.

title: Malaysia Visa - Ceysaid
text:
# Malaysia Visa

### **Visa Requirements for Travelers**

To apply for a **Malaysia Visa**, applicants must provide the following documents:

1. **Passport Requirements**
   * Passport must be valid for **at least seven (7) months** from the expected date of arrival in Malaysia.
2. **Photograph**
   * **Recent passport-size photo** with a **white background**.
3. **Passport Copy**
   * A **clear scanned copy** of the **passport bio-data page**.
4. **Previous Travel History**
   * Details of previous international travel, if applicable.
5. **Return Flight Ticket**
   * A confirmed **return ticket** (Maximum stay: **30 days in Malaysia**).
6. **Hotel Booking Confirmation**
   * Proof of **accommodation booking** for the stay in Malaysia.
7. **Financial Proof**
   * **Latest 3-month bank statement** & a **balance confirmation letter**.
   * **First-time travelers** must have a minimum bank balance of **$500**, verified with a **bank confirmation letter**.
8. **Visa Request Letter**
   * A **typed and signed letter** from the applicant requesting the visa.
9. **Employment or Business Documentation**
   * **Employment Letter** from the employer **OR**
   * **Business Registration Document** (Sinhala & English Translation) for business owners.
10. **Marriage Certificate** (If applicable)

* Must be translated into **Sinhala and English**.

11. **For Applicants Below 12 Years Old**

* **Birth Certificate** (Sinhala & English Translation) is **mandatory**.

### **Processing Time**

* **Standard Processing:** **2 working days**.
* If additional documents are requested by the visa officer, processing may be delayed by **2 more days**.
* Applicants must **submit any requested documents within 5 days**; failure to do so may result in visa rejection.

### **Important Notes**

* **Visa fees are non-refundable**, even if the application is rejected.
* **eVISA applications must be submitted from outside Malaysia** (except for applicants from Israel & North Korea, who are not eligible).
* If you are from a **non-eVISA eligible country**, you must **apply at the nearest Malaysian High Commission, Embassy, Consulate General, or Malaysia Visa Center**.

title: Japan Visa - Ceysaid
text:
# Japan Visa

### **Required Documents for Visa Application**

1. **Visa Application Form**
   * Each applicant, including children, must submit a separate form.
   * The form should be **filled in block letters** (typed or clearly handwritten).
   * The applicant’s name must match the **passport details**.
2. **Photograph**
   * **Size:** 2-inch x 1.4-inch (45mm x 35mm).
   * **Full-face view** on a **white or light-colored background**.
   * Taken within the **last 6 months**.
3. **Valid Passport**
   * Name and details should match the **National Identity Card (NIC)**.
   * **Two copies** of the **passport bio-data page**.
   * **One copy** of the **alteration page**, if applicable.
   * Must have at least **two blank pages**.
4. **Schedule of Stay**
   * A **detailed day-by-day itinerary** of activities in Japan.
5. **Accommodation Proof**
   * Hotel booking confirmation or a **sponsor’s residence certificate** if staying with a host.
6. **Travel Itinerary**
   * **Round-trip flight reservation** (It is recommended not to purchase tickets before visa approval).
7. **Employment & Financial Documents**
   * **Employed Applicants:**
     + A **letter from the employer** stating **employment start date** and **approved leave period**.
   * **Self-Employed Applicants:**
     + **Business registration document** (Original).
     + **Company Bank Statement** (Last 3 months, Original).
   * **Students:**
     + A **letter from the school or university** confirming the approved leave period.
8. **Personal Documents** (Mandatory)
   * **National Identity Card (NIC)**.
   * **Birth Certificate**.
   * **Marriage Certificate / Divorce Certificate / Death Certificate** (if applicable).
   * **Affidavit** (if there is a name mismatch in official documents).
9. **Personal Financial Records (Last 3 months)**
   * **Salary Slips**.
   * **Bank Statement & Bank Balance Confirmation Letter** (for current and savings accounts).
   * **Fixed Deposit** balance confirmation (if applicable).
10. **Previous Travel History to Japan (Last One Year)**

* Dates and duration of previous visits, if applicable.

### **Additional Notes**

* A **request letter** must be submitted individually by each applicant, stating they have applied through a travel agent.
* **Retired government employees** must submit **retirement documents**.
* **Non-government employees** should provide a **recommendation letter** explaining their employment status and source of funds.
* All **documents should be sorted** according to the checklist.
* **Original documents and A4-sized photocopies** must be submitted separately.
* **Documents in Sinhala or Tamil** must be accompanied by an **official English translation** by a sworn translator.
* **Do not use pins, staples, paper clips, or cello tape** on documents.
* The standard **processing time is 5 working days**, but additional scrutiny may extend this period.
* **Visa fees are non-refundable**, even if the application is rejected.

title: 07 Days Vietnam Tour Packages from Sri Lanka 2025
text:
# 07 Days Vietnam Tour Packages

*event* November - March  
*schedule* 7 days

* [Classic](https://ceysaid.com/tour-tag/classic/)
* [Family](https://ceysaid.com/tour-tag/family/)
* [Island Hoppping](https://ceysaid.com/tour-tag/island-hoppping/)

## 07 Days Vietnam Tour Packages

* **DAY 01: HANOI ARRIVAL (L,D)**

Arrive at Noi Bai International Airport, welcome by our guide and transfer to your hotel. The transfer distance is approximately 40 munites. Hanoi or “River-bound City” as its name literally means is Vietnam’s capital with a thousand-year-old history dating back to 1010. The city’s fascinating beauty which is characterized by unique cultural relics, ancient streets of antique houses, elegant French colonial buildings, tree-lined boulevards and romantic lakes has made it one of the most charming cities of Asia. Dinner at local restaurant. Then you will be transferred back to hotel and free at leisure by your own.  
Overnight in Hanoi.

**Meals:** Lunch and Dinner.  
**Accommodation:** A hotel used in Hanoi.

* **DAY 02: HANOI FULL DAY CITY TOUR (B, L, D)**

**Breakfast** at hotel.  
Morning: Visit Ho Chi Minh Complex which features the life of Ho Chi Minh, the nation’s founder. At the complex, visit the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, the colonial Presidential Palace, the One Pillar Pagoda. Continue to the Temple of Literature, known as the first university of Vietnam built in 1070 by the Ly Dynasty to honor Confucius, sages and outstanding Vietnamese scholars.  
Lunch at local restaurant.

Afternoon: continue the tour to one of the following museums of choice namely: the Museum of Fine Arts, the National Museum of Vietnamese History, the Museum of Revolution, the Museum of Military History or the Museum of Ethnology. Late afternoon, visit Hoan Kiem Lake and Ngoc Son Temple located on an islet in the lake. Hoan Kiem Lake is the center point of Hanoi and home to a rare endangered giant turtle species. Its name “Returned Sword” comes from a legend of a sacred sword being lent to an ancient Vietnamese King by a holy turtle from the lake and later returned to the turtle. Shop or walk at leisure in  
the Old Quarter of 36 ancient streets. Dinner at local restaurant. Free at leisure by your own. Overnight in Hanoi.

**Meals:** Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner.  
**Accommodation:** A hotel used in Hanoi.

* **DAY 03: HANOI – HAI PHONG – HALONG BAY (B, L, D)**

**Breakfast** at hotel.  
08:45 – 9:15: You are picked up from your hotel in Hanoi for 2.5 hour driving on 5B highway to the Got Harbor in Hai Phong Province for the cruise.  
12:00 – 12:30: Tender will transfer you to embark Calypso Cruises. You will be served with a welcome drink and a towel. Followed by our cruise briefing where you will also be given safety instructions before checking into your cabins. While the cruise sailing, you enjoy a bon-appetite lunch and being impressed by the beauty of hundreds of karst islands on your way passing Halong Bay to Lan Ha Bay.  
15:00: Visit Light and Dark Water Cave, where you can do kayaking or bamboo boat rowed by local people.  
17:30: Return to Calypso Cruise. It’s time to admire the magnificent sunset over the Gulf of Tonkin and  
enjoy happy hour (buy 2, get 1 more free). You take part in cooking demonstration to learn our Vietnamese cuisine.  
19:00: A great dinner awaits you.  
20:30 – 22:00: Your free time to enjoy the landscapes of the Bay on the top deck, sing karaoke, enjoy ordered drinks from the bar, try your luck at squid fishing or just relax in your cabins, etc. Overnight on board.

**Meals:** Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner  
**Accommodation:** A cruise used in Ha Long.

* **DAY 04: HA LONG BAY –– HANOI – FLIGHT TO HO CHI MINH (B, L, D)**

06:30: Wake up early to enjoy the dawn and start a perfect new day with Tai Chi exercise on the sundeck.

7:00 – 9:00: Have a light breakfast in the restaurant.

9:00: Check out your cabin. Then you take a bus route for about 20 minutes on Cat Ba Island from Gia Luan Pier to visit Army Hospital Cave, a special place to save the significant Vietnamese history during the resistance war. During the travel, you will be immersed in the wild fresh natural scenery of Cat Ba National Park.

10:30:Back to Calypso Cruises. You have lunch while the Calypso Cruise back to the harbor

12:00: Disembark the boat and return to Hanoi.

title: 07 Days Vietnam Tour Packages from Sri Lanka 2025
text:
10:30:Back to Calypso Cruises. You have lunch while the Calypso Cruise back to the harbor

12:00: Disembark the boat and return to Hanoi.

14:30 – 15:00: Arrive at Hanoi. End of the trip. Disembark and transfer back to Hanoi airport for flight to Ho Chi Minh Arrive in Saigon city, welcome by our guide and transfer to hotel for check in. Ho Chi Minh City formerly named Saigon is the largest city and economic center of Vietnam always bustled with activities of modern life. It is where businesses converge and shoppers indulge themselves into unlimited choices. However the city also carries with it a rich historical legacy in contrast with its dazzling glamor.  
Dinner at local restaurant. Back to hotel and free at leisure. Overnight in Ho Chi Minh.

**Meals:** Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner.  
**Accommodation:** A hotel used in Ho Chi Minh.

* **DAY 05: HO CHI MINH FULL DAY CU CHI TUNNEL AND CITY TOUR (B, L, D)**

**Breakfast** at hotel.  
Morning, take a half-day tour to the Cu Chi Tunnels, 75 km Northwest of downtown Ho Chi Minh City, which were once a major underground hideout and resistance base of Viet Cong forces during the two wars against the French and later on Americans. The tunnels, entirely hand-dug, formed a highly intricate network of interlinked multilevel passageways at times stretched as far as the Cambodian border and totaling over 120 km in length. Its complexity was beyond imagination containing meeting rooms, kitchens, wells, clinics, schools, depots, trenches and emergency exits all aimed for guerrilla warfare. The tunnels were deep and strong enough to withstand destructive bombings and self-contained enough to outlast prolonged sieges. Back to Ho Chi Minh. Lunch at local restaurant. Afternoon, take a half-day city tour to visit outside of Saigon Notre-Dame Basilica (is renovating until 2022), a neo-Roman cathedral built by the colonial French with materials entirely imported from Marseilles, Saigon Central Post Office, which was designed and constructed in the early 20th Century by the famous French architect Gustave Eiffel. Then, visit the War Remnants Museum, which features an extensive collection of exhibits including weaponry, photos and documentation on Vietnam’s wars with the French and Americans. End of day, you have free time to go shopping at Ben Thanh Market. Dinner at local restaurant. Free at leisure by your own and enjoy the night life. Overnight in Ho Chi Minh.

**Meals:** Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner.  
**Accommodation:** A hotel used in Ho Chi Minh.

* **DAY 6: HO CHI MINH – FULL DAY MYTHO (MEKONG DELTA) 65km–2hour driving (B, L, D)**

Morning, after breakfast, travel by road to the Mekong Delta, the world’s largest delta and Vietnam's largest rice bowl. On arrival in My Tho City, capital of Tieng Giang Provine, embark on a boat and navigate through intricate canals while observing the local daily life up close as well as the landscape of waterways and lush islands. Land on the Unicorn Island to enjoy wandering through verdant orchards, tasting freshfruits and listening to some Southern style traditional music. Move on to the second delta province, Ben Tre, where you will watch local villagers make coconut candy in a rural workshop, walk around briefly and try riding a horse-pulled carriage. More cruising to some bee-keeping households to taste fresh homegrown honey and honey tea. Lunch at local restaurant before going back to Ho Chi Minh City. Reach hotel and spend time at leisure. Dinner at Iocal restaurant. Overnight Ho Chi Minh.

**Meals:** Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner.  
**Accommodation:** A hotel used in Ho Chi Minh.

* **DAY 07: HO CHI MINH DEPARTURE (B)**

**Breakfast** at hotel.  
Free at leisure until transfer to airport for departure flight.

## Activities

**What your tour price includes**

* **Return air ticket(International )**
* One Way Air ticket ( Hanoi to Ho Chi Minh City)
* 05 Night hotel stay (twin Sharing) with breakfast
* 01 Night overnight cruise stay(twin sharing) in Halong Bay

  not include.)

* gratuities and personal expenses
* All other service not stated in the program

title: 11 Days Vietnam Tour Package - Ceysaid
text:
# 11 Days Vietnam Tour Package

*event* November - March  
*schedule* 11 days

* [Adventure](https://ceysaid.com/tour-tag/adventure/)
* [Classic](https://ceysaid.com/tour-tag/classic/)
* [Family](https://ceysaid.com/tour-tag/family/)
* [Free and Easy](https://ceysaid.com/tour-tag/free-and-easy/)

## 11 Days Vietnam Tour Package

**DAY 01 – HANOI ARRIVAL – HALF DAY CITY TOUR (L, D)**

Arrive at Noi Bai International Airport, welcome by our guide, and transfer to your hotel (check-in time at **14:00**).  
Lunch at a local restaurant.  
**14:30:** take a half-day tour to visit Hanoi train street (1 cup of coffee is included) - A unique attraction where visitors can watch a train speeding by along a cramped thoroughfare. Then continue to visit the Ho Chi Minh Complex (outside only) which features the life of Ho Chi Minh, the nation’s founder. Next visit Hoan Kiem Lake and Ngoc Son Temple located on an islet in the lake. Hoan Kiem Lake is the center point of Hanoi and home to a rare endangered giant turtle species. Its name “Returned Sword” comes from a legend of a sacred sword being lent to an ancient Vietnamese King by a holy turtle from the lake and later returned to the turtle.Get on green car (electric car) to drive leisurely in the Old Quarter of 36 ancient streets. Dinner at a local restaurant.T. Back to hotel and free at leisure.  
Overnight in Hanoi.  
**Meals:** Lunch, Dinner.  
**Accommodation:** A hotel used in Hanoi.

**DAY 02 – HANOI – HOA LU & TAM COC IN NINH BINH - HANOI (B, L, D)**

Breakfast at the hotel.  
At **09:00** am, pick up at your hotel and head out for Ninh Binh (2 hours driving). There is a beautiful roadside scenery on the way. Arriving in Ninh Binh City, first pay a visit to Hoa Lu – the ancient Royal Capital of Vietnam, visit the two temples: the Temple of King Dinh and King Le, and admire the unique architecture from ancient times. Lunch at a local restaurant.  
In the afternoon, board a small sampan to visit Tam Coc which means literally “three caves” (2.5-hour boat trip). With Halong bay-type views of majestic limestone cliffs and mysterious caves, as well as countryside landscapes like peaceful streams, rice paddies, and deserted green fields, Tam Coc has it all. You will feel the fresh air and peaceful moments, a very good opportunity for photo lovers, freezing the special moments and scenery.After that, transfer back to the hotel in Hanoi. Dinner at a local restaurant.  
Free at leisure on your own.Overnight in Hanoi.  
**Meal:** Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner.  
**Hotel accommodation:** a hotel used in Hanoi.

**DAY 03 – HANOI – PRIVATE TRANSFER TO SAPA TOWN – CAT VILLAGE (B, L, D)**

Breakfast at the hotel.  
**07:00:** The guide and private bus will pick you up from Hotel Sapa Town.  
Reach Sapa Town after a 5.5 – 6 hour drive.Lunch at a local restaurant. Check-in the hotel and take a rest after a long journey.  
**14:30 OR 15:00:** A local guide and private vehicle will take you 3 km out of Sapa through a hilly landscape to Cat Cat village, home to a community of the Hmong ethnic minority people. The traditional trades of brocade making and silver and bronze smith are still practiced nowadays by villagers after numerous generations of their ancestors. You can see Hmong women in their looms weaving fabrics. Visit Tien Sa waterfall where a French-built hydropower station has been in service since 1920. Back to hotel and free.  
**19:00:** depart for dinner at a local restaurant.After dinner, the guide and driver escort you back to the hotel.Free at leisure to explore Sapa Town at night.  
**Meals:** Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner.  
**Accommodation:** A hotel used in Sapa.

**DAY 04 – SAPA - HEAVEN GATE & O QUY HO PASS – TRANSFER BACK TO HANOI (B, L, D)**

title: 11 Days Vietnam Tour Package - Ceysaid
text:
**DAY 04 – SAPA - HEAVEN GATE & O QUY HO PASS – TRANSFER BACK TO HANOI (B, L, D)**

Breakfast at the hotel.  
**08:30:** A local guide and private vehicle will escort you to Heaven Gate – Tram Ton Pass. At 2,047m above sea level, the Heaven Gate – Tram Ton Pass is the highest mountain pass in Vietnam. From here enjoy walking around for half an hour for the beautiful scenery of both two sides of the Hoang Lien Son Mountain range. The wind blows stronger to force the clouds to move constantly. It is the best point here to enjoy the breathtaking scenery of the two sides of the Fansipan Mountain range. Amazingly you can feel the weather very differently on the sides: On many days It is foggy and cloudy on the Sapa side and sunny brightly on the other side.Leaving the Heaven Gate for the way back downhill 7km, then stop to visit some lush farm in O Quy Ho where different kinds of vegetables, flowers, fruit trees, and even medicinal plants have grown for years which makes this part of Sapa evergreen.Transfer back to the town center to have lunch at a local restaurant.**14:00:** leaving Sapa Town to Hanoi.Reach Hanoi after a 5.5 - 6 hour drive.Dinner at a local restaurant.Free and overnight in Hanoi.  
**Meal:** Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner.  
**Hotel accommodation:** a hotel used in Hanoi.

**DAY 05 – HANOI – HALONG BAY (B, L, D)**

Breakfast at the hotel.  
**09:00:** The guide and bus will pick you up from the hotel to Tuan Chau Harbor via the new highway.  
**12:00:** Receptionists welcome guests at the International Tuan Chau harbor.  
**12:45:** Captain welcomes guests to cruises by tender. Check in cruise, whilst enjoying a welcome drink, safety briefing, and check-in cabin.  
**13:00:** Leaving International Tuan Chau harbor, discover the Halong Bay - natural wonders of the world. Have lunch with special foods while cruising through famous sights Ba Hang fishing village, Đỉnh Hương island, Chó Đá islet, Gà Chọi islet...  
**14:30:** Cruises stop at Bồ Nâu cave, the core area of the world's natural heritage. Transfer by tender to Surprise Cave - one of the most beautiful caves in Halong Bay.  
**15:45:** Leaving Surprise cave, transfer by tender to the Titov island was named by Uncle Ho, after Gherman Stepanovich Titov - the Soviet astronaut who visited in 1962 Enjoy swimming, relaxing on the beach, or climbing the steps of the Rock to the top of the island. On the top of the mountain, you can see the corner of magical Halong Bay.  
...  
**16:45:** Back to the cruise. Relax and enjoy the sunset on the sun deck. Go to the overnight place.  
**18:00 – 19:00:** HAPPY HOUR - Buy 1 drink get 1 free (wine excluded).  
**18:30:** Enjoy the Vietnamese cooking demonstration.  
**19:00:** Acquaint your seat with the set menu dinner.  
**20:30:** Evening activities include: film entertainment (in the dining room); squid fishing; and massage (contact with cruise manager for service price)...  
Overnight on board.  
**Meals:** Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner.  
**Accommodation:** A cruise used in Ha Long.

**DAY 06 – HA LONG BAY – HANOI – FLIGHT TO DA NANG (B, BRUNCH, D)**

**06:00:** Morning call (if you request).  
**06:30:** Enjoy Tai Chi instructions on the sundeck in 30 minutes whilst cruising to Luồn cave.  
**07:00:** Enjoy a light breakfast in the restaurant (coffee, tea, fresh milk, water, pastries, bread, egg, fresh fruit, cereals will be served in the dining room).  
**08:00:** After breakfast, take a guided tour to visit Luồn cave - one of the caves open to the form beautiful endemic vegetation of Halong Bay. Visit Luồn cave by a traditionally woven (bamboo) boat rowed by a local boatman.  
**08:30:** Then come back to cruise, relax, and check out of the cabin. Please put your luggage outside the door. Our porters will collect and get them ready on the Pier for you. Finally, the cruise disembarks in International Tuan Chau harbor.  
**09:15:** Enjoy the brunch buffet menu served in the dining room. Settle bills in the dining room after brunch (if you have one).  
**10:45:** Transfer by tender back to Tuan Chau harbor and disembarkation. Farewell to guests. Get on the bus to transfer back to Noi Bai airport  
**14:30 – 15:00:** reach the airport and take a flight to Da Nang.  
Upon arrival, a local guide and driver will pick you up from the airport to the restaurant for dinner. Then transfer to the hotel for check-in. On the way, pass by Dragon Bridge – a symbol of Da Nang beach city.  
Free and overnight in Da Nang.  
**Meals:** Breakfast, Brunch, Dinner.  
**Accommodation:** A hotel used in Da Nang.

**DAY 07 – DA NANG - BA NA HILLS – GOLDEN BRIDGE – DA NANG (B, L, D)**

title: 11 Days Vietnam Tour Package - Ceysaid
text:
**DAY 07 – DA NANG - BA NA HILLS – GOLDEN BRIDGE – DA NANG (B, L, D)**

Breakfast at the hotel.  
**09:00:** you will visit Ba Na Hills which is far from Da Nang center about 35km. Upon arrival, take a Cable Car which gains 5 world scores to Ba Na Hills station. Stop at By Night station to visit Vong Nguyet Hills, Linh Ung pagoda, and The Old Villas of French. Continue moving up by cable car to the Nui Chua Mountain – the top of Ba Na range, visiting Nginh Phong top, Le Nim Villas, Orchid Garden… Experience the hill climbing train (the first and unique funicular in Vietnam) and visit the flower garden Le Jardin D’amour and Linh Ung Pagoda.Buffet lunch in Ba Na Hills.Free for joining games in Fantasy Park – the third biggest indoor games zone in Vietnam with a series of interesting games: 4 – 5D film, skiver, The Death Race, Dinosaur Park…There is a bridge that is brand new and was opened to the public in June 2018. It is known as the Golden Bridge, and it stands 1,400m above sea level above the Ba Na hills. The gold-colored walkway is lined with purple Lobelia Chrysanthemums. Offering majestic views of the surrounding countryside. It is seemingly being held aloft by two giant hands.Then take the cable car down. Transfer back to the hotel in Da Nang along the beach road.Dinner at a local restaurant. Free and overnight in Da Nang.  
**Meals:** Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner.  
**Accommodation:** A hotel used in Da Nang.

**Note:** Cable car ticket includes all free games in Fantasy Park: over 105 games and free Knight Game (Chute), entrance fees for Funicular, The Golden Bridge.

**DAY 08 – DA NANG – CAM THANH COCONUT VILLAGE – HOI AN ANCIENT TOWN TOUR (B, L, D)**

Breakfast at the hotel.  
Morning free to relax on the beach/ swimming.**12:00:** lunch at a local restaurant.  
After that, depart to Cam Thanh Village. Here you will have a chance to see the locals hardly catching fish in some traditional way on the river. We have 45 minutes to try basketboats with local people. You will be immersed in the natural beauty of the Nipa palm forest.  
Back to the dock. Continue to visit Hoi An Ancient Town. It is more interesting in the late afternoon. There is plenty to see in this delightful town, some of which are the famous Japanese Covered Bridges, Pottery Museum, 200-year old Tan Ky House, Chua Ong Pagoda, and vibrant market. Today we include these on a walking tour past many low tiled houses scattered along the small streets and assembly halls, which reflect the town's multi-cultural past. The rest of the day is free for leisure to enjoy the shopping in Hoi An – a paradise for silk & fashionable clothing and accessories.Dinner at a local restaurant.Free and overnight in Da Nang.  
**Meals:** Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner.  
**Accommodation:** A hotel used in Da Nang.

**DAY 09 – DA NANG - FLIGHT TO HO CHI MINH – HALF DAY CITY TOUR (B, L, D)**

Breakfast at the hotel.  
Guide and driver escort you back to the airport for a flight to Ho Chi Minh.Upon arrival, a local guide and driver pick you up from the airport to the city center.Have lunch at a local restaurant.Afternoon, take a half-day city tour to visit outside of Saigon Notre-Dame Basilica (is renovating in 2022), a neo Roman cathedral built by the colonial French with materials entirely imported from Marseilles, Saigon Central Post Office, which was designed and constructed in the early 20th Century by the famous French architect Gustave Eiffel.Then, visit the War Remnants Museum, which features an extensive collection of exhibits including weaponry, photos, and documentation on Vietnam’s wars with the French and Americans. End of the day, you have free time to go shopping at Ben Thanh Market.Dinner at a local restaurant.  
Free at leisure on your own and enjoy the nightlife.  
Overnight in Ho Chi Minh.  
**Meals:** Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner.  
**Accommodation:** A hotel used in Ho Chi Minh.

**DAY 10 – HO CHI MINH – MEKONG DELTA (MY THO) – HO CHI MINH (B, L, D)**

title: 11 Days Vietnam Tour Package - Ceysaid
text:
**DAY 10 – HO CHI MINH – MEKONG DELTA (MY THO) – HO CHI MINH (B, L, D)**

Breakfast at the hotel.  
**09:00:** The guide and driver pick you up from the hotel lobby. Transfer to the Mekong Delta, the world’s largest delta and Vietnam’s largest rice bowl.On arrival in My Tho City after a 2-hour drive, board a motorboat and navigate through intricate canals while observing the local daily life up close as well as the landscape of waterways and lush islands. Land on Thoi Son Island where you can listen to Southern traditional music performed by native artists. Next, you will change to a Sampan boat to cruise through the narrow canals and visit households with traditional occupations of home-based coconut candy and rice-wine making. Continue travel by motorized boat to another island to visit a bee farm taste tea with fresh honey from the beehives and enjoy fresh fruits. Interact with residents to understand about the river life. Their economy is based mostly on tourism, mangoes, bananas, and citrus fruit.  
Lunch at an Indian restaurant.On the return to My Tho, stop to visit the Vinh Trang Pagoda, a prominent local Buddhist structure built in 1850 with a unique architectural mixture of Vietnamese, Roman, Chinese, and Cambodian styles characterized by a highly ornate façade.Reach the hotel at 17:00. Free at leisure.  
**19:30:** dinner at Iocal restaurant.  
After dinner, on your own, explore “Little Tokyo” with Japanese-owned pubs, barbecue restaurants, and massage services abound. Japanese signage overwhelms Vietnamese translations. Japanese style is maintained throughout the neighborhood, including in each business’s inter. Overnight in Ho Chi Minh.  
**Meals:** Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner.  
**Accommodation:** A hotel used in Ho Chi Minh.

**DAY 11 – HO CHI MINH - DEPARTURE (B)**

Breakfast at the hotel.  
This morning you will be transferred to the airport for your flight back home or your next destination.  
**Meal:** Breakfast.

## Activities
